title: eleocharis rostellata
intro: Eleocharis rostellata, also known as dwarf spike-rush, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its rhizomes, which contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Eleocharis rostellata include its potential use in treating fever, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and relieve stomach pain. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and can be used fresh or dried, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
